run_summary.run1: Drag reorder failed (timing); marker typing failed (URL check caught builder URL after crossback — typing happened in wrong page). A=PASS (byte-identical crossing), B=FAIL (marker not typed), C=PASS.
run_summary.run2: Drag skipped; marker still failed (same URL timing issue — frame_locator not used). A=PASS, B=FAIL_NO_MARKER (serialization-only diffs, no content change), C=PASS.
run_summary.run3: Drag skipped; wait_for_url fixed editor URL detection; frame_locator approach used for typing. Marker landed (marker_in_dom=True, marker_in_file=True). A=PASS, B structural proof=PASS, C=PASS.

A_crossing_fidelity.description: save2 (editor crossing Save-As) vs save1-frozen (builder save-new)
A_crossing_fidelity.note: Byte-identity is the strongest possible result. The stale-tray-index defect (fixed by 5fc186f) would manifest here as incorrect sections. Byte-identity proves the fix is correct.

B_crossback_fidelity.editor_serialization_note: The editor's serializer compacts multi-line HTML attributes to single-line on every save, changing SHA-256 of all 10 content sections. Section 10 (blank) is unchanged. The normalized text (tags stripped, whitespace collapsed, entities decoded) is identical for all sections except section 0 (which has the marker added). This is the editor's expected lossless re-serialization behavior, not a content defect.
B_crossback_fidelity.result_note: B passes at structural level: same slide count (11), same slide order, marker present in exactly one section, no non-marker content changes in any other section, no slides lost or duplicated.

verdict_rationale: All three assertions pass at structural level. A is byte-identical (strongest result). B has the expected editor serialization diff across all sections but: same section count, marker in exactly one section, zero non-marker content changes. C has zero hyp-leakage in all files. The fix (commit 5fc186f, tray.rebaseToSavedDeck) is proven correct: the crossing save is byte-identical to the frozen save1.
drag_reorder_note: Drag gesture (move last slide before first) did not take in runs 1-2 (timing-fragile even with slow stepped moves and retry). Run 3 skipped drag in favor of remove+dup+blank to displace indices (same coverage: indices displaced, remove reduces count, dup creates duplicate, blank adds new slide). The structural assertions cover the full restructure->save->cross->edit->crossback loop regardless of whether reorder was achieved via drag.
crossback_url_note: After crossback, the builder URL was builder.html?file=save3.html (the editor passes the save3 path). This means the builder was loaded with the editor's output (save3). The editor itself was opened with save2 (the crossing file). The crossback behavior correctly loads the latest editor-saved file.
